message.text:
The sentry-python SDK has a function called `push_scope` that has been soft-deprecated since version 2.0.0. The problem is that calling it doesn't actually warn the user at all — no deprecation notice, nothing. Users have no idea they should be migrating away from it. The fix should make `push_scope` emit a `DeprecationWarning` whenever it's called (both when used as a context manager and when called with a callback), and the warning message should tell users it'll be removed in the next major version and point them to the migration guide at https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/python/migration/1.x-to-2.x#scope-pushing.

Deliverables: the source code is updated so calling `push_scope` triggers the expected deprecation notice; CHANGELOG.md has a new entry describing this change; the `push_scope` docstring is updated to mention that it's deprecated and will be removed; all tests in `tests/test_push_scope_deprecation_warn.py` and `tests/check_quality.py` pass; and the rest of the test suite (including `tests/test_basics.py` and `tests/test_api.py`) stays green. Modify the content or dependency based on the terminal output until all tests pass.

Please create your own Python virtual environment, install the package in editable mode along with the test dependencies, then run the test files to verify everything works.

The current working directory is not a git repository . When reading or writing any file, always open it in Python with `encoding='utf-8'` explicitly; don't use PowerShell's Set-Content or Out-File because they default to ANSI encoding and will silently corrupt Unicode characters.
